006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
020
021import java.text.*;
022import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
023import java.util.function.*;
024import java.util.logging.*;
025
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.logging.Logger;
027import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
029
030import jakarta.servlet.*;
031import jakarta.servlet.http.*;
032
033/**
034 * Identical to {@link RestServlet} but doesn't extend from {@link HttpServlet}.
035 *
036 * <p>
037 * This is particularly useful in Spring Boot environments that auto-detect servlets to deploy in servlet containers,
038 * but you want this resource to be deployed as a child instead.
039 *
040 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
041 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestAnnotatedClassBasics">@Rest-Annotated Class Basics</a>
042 * </ul>
043 */
044public abstract class RestResource {
045
046   private AtomicReference<RestContext> context = new AtomicReference<>();
047
048   /**
049    * The programmatic configuration builder stashed on this instance, or <jk>null</jk> when the
050    * resource was constructed without a builder.  Mutable so it can be written by either the
051    * {@link #RestResource(RestBuilder)} constructor or {@link Builder#build()}.  Read non-reflectively by
052    * {@link RestContext} during construction so builder-supplied values take precedence over {@code @Rest}
053    * annotation values.
054    */
055   RestBuilder<?> restBuilder;
056
057   /**
058    * Default constructor.
059    */
060   protected RestResource() {}
061
062   /**
063    * Builder-injection constructor.
064    *
065    * @param builder The programmatic configuration builder.  May be <jk>null</jk>.
066    */
067   protected RestResource(RestBuilder<?> builder) {
068      this.restBuilder = builder;
069   }
070
071   /**
072    * Returns the programmatic configuration builder stashed on this resource, or <jk>null</jk> if none.
073    *
074    * @return The stashed builder, or <jk>null</jk>.
075    */
076   @SuppressWarnings({
077      "java:S1452" // RestBuilder<?> wildcard return intentional; concrete builder type varies by REST resource class
078   })
079   public RestBuilder<?> getRestBuilder() {
080      return restBuilder;
081   }
082
083   /**
084    * Creates a new fluent builder for programmatically configuring an instance of the specified resource type.
085    *
086    * @param <R> The resource type.
087    * @param type The resource type to build.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
088    * @return A new builder.
089    */
090   public static <R extends RestResource> DefaultBuilder<R> builder(Class<R> type) {
091      return new DefaultBuilder<>(type);
092   }
093
094   /**
095    * Returns the current thread-local HTTP request.
096    *
097    * @return The current thread-local HTTP request, or <jk>null</jk> if it wasn't created.
098    */
099   public synchronized RestRequest getRequest() { return getContext().getLocalSession().getOpSession().getRequest(); }
100
101   /**
102    * Returns the current thread-local HTTP response.
103    *
104    * @return The current thread-local HTTP response, or <jk>null</jk> if it wasn't created.
105    */
106   public synchronized RestResponse getResponse() { return getContext().getLocalSession().getOpSession().getResponse(); }
107
108   /**
109    * Log a message.
110    *
111    * <p>
112    * Subclasses can intercept the handling of these messages by overriding {@link #doLog(Level, Throwable, Supplier)}.
113    *
114    * @param level The log level.
115    * @param msg The message to log.
116    * @param args Optional {@link MessageFormat}-style arguments.
117    */
118   public void log(Level level, String msg, Object...args) {
119      doLog(level, null, fs(msg, args));
120   }
121
122   /**
123    * Log a message.
124    *
125    * <p>
126    * Subclasses can intercept the handling of these messages by overriding {@link #doLog(Level, Throwable, Supplier)}.
127    *
128    * @param level The log level.
129    * @param cause The cause.
130    * @param msg The message to log.
131    * @param args Optional {@link MessageFormat}-style arguments.
132    */
133   public void log(Level level, Throwable cause, String msg, Object...args) {
134      doLog(level, cause, fs(msg, args));
135   }
136
137   /**
138    * Main logger method.
139    *
140    * <p>
141    * The default behavior logs a message to the Java logger of the class name.
142    *
143    * <p>
144    * Subclasses can override this method to implement their own logger handling.
145    *
146    * @param level The log level.
147    * @param cause Optional throwable.
148    * @param msg The message to log.
149    */
150   protected void doLog(Level level, Throwable cause, Supplier<String> msg) {
151      var c = context.get();
152      var logger = c == null ? null : c.getLogger();
153      if (logger == null)
154         logger = Logger.getLogger(cn(this));
155      logger.log(level, cause, msg);
156   }
157
158   /**
159    * Returns the read-only context object that contains all the configuration information about this resource.
160    *
161    * @return The context information on this servlet.
162    */
163   protected RestContext getContext() {
164      if (context.get() == null)
165         throw new InternalServerError("RestContext object not set on resource.");
166      return context.get();
167   }
168
169   /**
170    * Returns the runtime-overridden top-level mount paths for this resource.
171    *
172    * <p>
173    * Subclasses can override this method to substitute the {@code @Rest(paths)} annotation defaults at
174    * construction time. This is the &quot;getter&quot; rung in the runtime-override resolution chain
175    * documented on {@link RestContext#getPaths()}:
176    * <ol>
177    *    <li>Programmatic ({@code RestContext.Builder.paths(String...)}) &mdash; highest precedence.
178    *    <li>This {@code getPaths()} getter (when non-{@code null}).
179    *    <li>{@code @Rest(paths={...})} annotation default (SVL-resolved per element, comma-split) &mdash;
180    *       lowest precedence.
181    * </ol>
182    *
183    * <h5 class='section'>Accepted return shapes:</h5><ul>
184    *    <li>{@code null} &mdash; no override; resolution falls through to the {@code @Rest(paths=...)}
185    *       annotation default.
186    *    <li>{@link String} &mdash; a single path or a comma-delimited list, e.g.
187    *       {@code "/healthz,/readyz"}.
188    *    <li>{@code String[]} &mdash; each element may itself be a comma-delimited list.
189    *    <li>{@link java.util.Collection Collection}, {@link java.util.List List},
190    *       {@link java.util.Set Set}, {@link Iterable}, or {@link java.util.stream.Stream Stream}
191    *       of any of the above (nested mixes are recursively flattened).
192    *    <li>Primitive arrays (boxed during flattening).
193    * </ul>
194    *
195    * <p>
196    * Non-{@link String} leaves are coerced via {@link String#valueOf(Object)}.  Each leaf string then runs
197    * through the same per-element pipeline used for {@code @Rest(paths=...)} annotation elements: SVL is
198    * applied via the bean-store-backed {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.VarResolverSession VarResolverSession}
199    * (so {@code $C{key}} consults the live {@link org.apache.juneau.config.Config Config}, {@code $E{NAME,default}}
200    * and {@code $S{prop,default}} resolve through the bootstrap variable catalog), and the post-SVL value is
201    * comma-split with each piece trimmed and empty pieces dropped.
202    *
203    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
204    *    <li class='note'>
205    *       Returning {@code null} (the default) means &quot;inherit annotation&quot; &mdash; the next rung resolves.
206    *    <li class='note'>
207    *       Returning {@code new String[0]}, an empty collection, an empty string, or a blank string means
208    *       &quot;explicitly clear&quot; &mdash; no top-level mounts.
209    * </ul>
210    *
211    * @return The runtime-overridden mount paths in any accepted shape, or {@code null} to inherit the
212    *    annotation default.
213    * @since 10.0.0
214    */
215   public Object getPaths() { return null; }
216
217   /**
218    * Sets the context object for this servlet.
219    *
220    * @param context Sets the context object on this servlet.
221    * @throws ServletException If error occurred during post-initialiation.
222    */
223   protected void setContext(RestContext context) throws ServletException {
224      this.context.set(context);
225   }
226
227   /**
228    * Fluent builder for programmatically configuring a {@link RestResource} subclass.
229    *
230    * <p>
231    * Subclassable, self-typed (CRTP) flavor builder.  For the common (non-subclassed) case
232    * use {@link RestResource#builder(Class)} which returns the concrete {@link DefaultBuilder} leaf.
233    *
234    * @param <R> The resource type produced by {@link #build()}.
235    * @param <SELF> The concrete builder type (self type).
236    */
237   @SuppressWarnings({
238      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
239   })
240   public static class Builder<R extends RestResource, SELF extends Builder<R, SELF>> extends AbstractRestBuilder<R, SELF> {
241
242      /**
243       * Constructor.
244       *
245       * @param type The resource type produced by {@link #build()}.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
246       */
247      protected Builder(Class<R> type) {
248         super(type);
249      }
250
251      @Override /* AbstractRestBuilder */
252      public R build() {
253         var r = createResource();
254         r.restBuilder = this;
255         return r;
256      }
257   }
258
259   /**
260    * Concrete default leaf builder returned by {@link RestResource#builder(Class)} for the common (non-subclassed)
261    * case.
262    *
263    * @param <R> The resource type produced by {@link #build()}.
264    */
265   public static final class DefaultBuilder<R extends RestResource> extends Builder<R, DefaultBuilder<R>> {
266      DefaultBuilder(Class<R> type) {
267         super(type);
268      }
269   }
270}
